repo.description: The most widely used Python to C compiler
repo.name: cython

body:
Take a fairly cautious approach here and only enable vectorcall for classes where we have complete visibility of the object creation (so disable it for anything external and anything from a pxd).

In the situations where we think it's worthwhile, I make the vectorcall-style versions the main implementation. Therefore the "args, kwds" `tp_new` and `tp_init` slots just defer to the vectorcall implementation.  This is probably a minor pessimization in the case where they are needed (mainly when inheriting from them with something that can't use vectorcall).

Some small microbenchmarking (of creating classes from Python) suggests it might make class creation between ~30-50% faster so is very likely worthwhile.

commit.message:
Enable vectorcall for cdef class creation

Take a fairly cautious approach here and only enable vectorcall
for classes where we have complete visibility of the object creation
(so disable it for anything external and anything from a pxd).

This involves a reasonable amount of code duplication:
* all the `tp_new` slots are duplicated with a
  vectorcall version.
* all the `__cinit__` and `__init__` functions in suitable cdef
  classes have an extra wrapper attached taking vectorcall
  arguments.

Some small microbenchmarking (of creating classes from Python)
suggests it might make class creation between ~30-50% faster
so is ver likely worthwhile.

Avoid duplicating wrapper functions

Instead use the vectorcall one as the master copy where we
believe it's useful.

Use static stack array of 5

matches what cpython does

title: Enable vectorcall for cdef class creation
